title: Fable 5 leaves Claude subscriptions today, moves to metered usage credits
subtitle: As of July 7, Anthropic's most capable model is no longer bundled into Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise plans — access requires per-token credits at $10 input and $50 output per million.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Anthropic's pricing change for Claude Fable 5 takes effect today. Through July 6, Pro, Max, Team, and select Enterprise plans included Fable 5 for up to fifty percent of a user's weekly limits. Starting July 7, that inclusion ends. Fable 5 requests now consume usage credits — a metered billing bucket separate from the subscription — at the confirmed API rate of ten dollars per million input tokens and fifty dollars per million output tokens. Users who have not enabled credits will find that Fable 5 prompts simply refuse to run on their plan. Sonnet 5 and Opus 4.8 remain inside the subscription limits.

The mechanism matters more than the price. Anthropic is separating the model tier from the plan tier. A subscription used to buy you a defined bucket of capacity across every model the lab shipped. Now the plan buys you the standard tier and the frontier tier is metered by the token. That is closer to the historical AWS billing model than to the historical SaaS billing model — a subtle but important shift in how frontier AI is packaged.

The trigger is capacity, not strategy. Fable 5 was restored globally on July 1 after export controls were lifted, but demand for the model has run well ahead of the compute Anthropic has to serve it. Bundling frontier access into a fixed-price plan works when supply is elastic. When it isn't, the lab has two options — throttle everyone equally or move the heaviest users onto usage-based pricing that reflects real cost. Anthropic has chosen the second path and told subscribers it plans to fold Fable 5 back into standard plans once capacity allows.

A takeaway for learners: if you are budgeting AI usage for a project, learn to read pricing pages carefully. The difference between a bundled tier and a metered tier is not a marketing footnote — it can be the difference between a fifty-dollar month and a fifty-thousand-dollar month. And when a lab moves its best model out of subscriptions, watch what happens next: usually it is a signal that inference demand has outrun capacity, and the same pattern shows up at the other frontier labs within weeks.

sources.title: Fable 5 Subscription Ends Tomorrow: Per-Token Costs and Who Gets Hit Hardest — TechTimes
Claude Fable 5 isn't permanently leaving subscriptions, Anthropic says — BleepingComputer
